steps.title: Apply the compound growth formula
steps.content:
Increases by 1% of its value the previous year indicates exponential growth. Each year, the stock value multiplies by $1.01$. Using the compound growth model with initial value $V_0 = 240$ and growth rate $r = 0.01$:
$$V(t) = 240(1.01)^t$$

steps.title: Identify the correct choice
steps.content:
• Choices A and B use linear multiplication ($1.01t$ and $1.1t$), which models adding a fixed amount each year, not compound growth. Eliminate.
  • Choice D correctly uses exponentiation, but the base is $1.1$, which represents a 10% annual growth rate, not 1%. Eliminate.
  • Choice C matches our formula: exponential form with base $1.01$ (representing 1% growth).

That matches choice C — C is correct.
